page: Microsoft Azure NC48ads_A100_v4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7V13 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 440.0 GB of system memory, providing 9.17 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via two integrated NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 160 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 2051 GB disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in Passmark memory testing and Geekbench image processing. The dual A100 GPUs deliver top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 501.25 tokens per second for prompt processing and 23.97 tokens per second for text generation on large 70B models. CPU-bound workloads show strong performance in stress-ng multi-core execution, Passmark CPU encryption, and Geekbench software compilation. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and single-threaded text processing fall into the average category.

Given the high density of GPU resources and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that heavily utilize parallel GPU compute and large memory footprints. The combination of 160 GB VRAM and 440 GB system memory makes it suitable for large language model inference, deep learning training, image processing, and ray tracing. It is also well-suited for database operations and Redis deployments, supported by strong multi-core CPU performance and local storage, though it may be over-provisioned for basic web serving or single-threaded tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure NC48ads_A100_v4 is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 7V13 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz, 440.0 GB of system memory, and 2051 GB of local storage. It features two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 160 GB of VRAM. In benchmark evaluations, the instance achieves top-tier results in LLM inference, particularly for large 70B models, and image processing. It also demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and database operations. However, large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded text processing remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is optimized for deep learning, large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, and memory-intensive database workloads where parallel GPU processing and dedicated CPU cores can be fully leveraged.
og_description: Microsoft Azure NC48ads_A100_v4 is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 440 GB RAM, 2051 GB local storage, and two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering top-tier LLM inference and image processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure NC48ads_A100_v4 features 48 AMD EPYC cores, 440 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA A100 GPUs for top-tier LLM inference and parallel computing.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 160 GB VRAM for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM
48 dedicated AMD EPYC 7V13 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
440.0 GB system memory and 2051 GB local storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
Strong multi-core CPU performance and database execution speeds
Suited for deep learning, image processing, and ray tracing
